enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

Who is the lead actor in the movie Mannadhi Mannan?

The lead actor is M. G. Ramachandran.

What is the theme of the song Achcham Enbadhu?

The song emphasizes courage and the importance of fulfilling one's duty.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music was composed by the legendary duo Viswanathan–Ramamoorthy.

What year was the movie Mannadhi Mannan released?

The movie was released in 1960.

What emotions does the song evoke?

The song evokes feelings of motivation and patriotism.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song Achcham Enbadhu from the movie Mannadhi Mannan, released in 1960, features the legendary actor M. G. Ramachandran and actress P. Bhanumathi. This motivational and patriotic song, composed by Viswanathan–Ramamoorthy with lyrics by Kannadasan, inspires listeners to overcome fear and embrace their responsibilities. The powerful rendition by the singer adds depth to its impactful message. This classic remains a cherished piece in Tamil cinema's musical heritage.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Achcham Enbadhu, a timeless classic from the movie Mannadhi Mannan, resonates with themes of courage and patriotism. Sung with heartfelt emotion, this song captures the essence of overcoming fear and embracing duty. Its powerful lyrics and melodious composition continue to inspire generations.
stanzaMeanings: Achcham Enbadhu Song Lyrics Nadodi Mannan
Fear is foolishness; courage is the wealth of the Dravidian people.
Fear is foolishness; courage is the wealth of the Dravidian people.
Even if death comes in six or a hundred forms, protecting the motherland is our duty.
Kanagavijarin adorned his head and placed a stone, Chera's son.
At the edge of the horizon, he raised the fish flag and sang praises, the Pandiyan.
In the body of the growing child, Tamil mother instills courage.
If turmoil arises, the child will rise to protect the honor of the mother.
Many have lived, many have passed, but who remains in the hearts of the people?
The great warriors who protect honor stand in history.
